---
title: "UGANDA'S DIGITAL MENTAL HEALTH LEAP. \n Harnessing DHIS2 for Data Driven Insights"
subtitle: "Data Analysis Report"
author: "Project team"
#date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    lot: true
    lof: true
    number_sections: true
    number-depth: 2
    code_folding: hide
    theme: cosmo
    highlight: tango
    df_print: paged
  docx:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
source(here::here("scripts","00_setup.R"))

library(gemini.R)
base::readRenviron("~/.Renviron")
api_key = Sys.getenv("GEMINI_API_KEY")
setAPI(api_key)
```

# EXPLORATION OF THE EVENTS DATA


```{r data_import}
#| label: import_data
#| include: false
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

full_data <- read_csv(here::here("data", "data_clean.csv"), col_names = T)
target_data <- full_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(mh_diagnosis_cleaned)) %>% 
  filter(mh_year>=2022 & mh_year<=2024) %>%
  mutate(
    mh_age_group = cut(mh_age_in_years, 
                             breaks = c(0, 4, 9, 14, 19, 35, 45, 55, Inf), 
                             labels = c("< 5yrs", "5-9", "10-14", "15-19",
                                        "20-35", "36-45", "46-55",  " > 55"),)
  ) %>% 
  group_by(patient_id) %>% 
  mutate(mh_visit_log = n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>%
  select(patient_id, mh_organisation_unit_name, mh_date_of_visit, mh_visit_log, mh_month,
         mh_year, mh_sex, mh_age_group, mh_classification, mh_diagnosis_cleaned, everything()
         )
```


## Data Sources and Coverage
Data used in this analysis was extracted from out-patient register records from 18 hospitals across Uganda - comprising of 17 regional referral hospitals and one national referral hospital. The data covers the period from 2022 to 2024 and includes individuals diagnosed with a range of mental health conditions.
@tbl-data-source presents the distribution of patient records across the participating hospitals highlighting the relative contribution of each facility to the overall dataset, while @fig-total-patients illustrates the geographical distribution of the participating health facilities across the country.
```{r}
#| label: tbl-data-source
#| tbl-cap: "Distribution of patient records across participating hospitals"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| caption: "Data Source"


# Data sources
target_data %>% 
  select(mh_organisation_unit_name) %>% 
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(
                         label = list( mh_organisation_unit_name = ""),
                         sort = list(mh_organisation_unit_name ~ "frequency"),
                          missing_text = "(Missing)") %>% 
modify_header(label ~ "**Hospital**") %>% 
modify_table_body(~ .x %>% slice(-1))

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-total-patients
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| fig-cap: "Hospital location and number of patient records from each hospital"

#Insert the map from plots folder

knitr::include_graphics(here("outputs", "uganda_mh_map.png"))
```



## Demographics
@tbl-table1-demo summarizes the patient demographic and clinical characteristics. A total of 142,716 unique patients were included in the analysis with an equal distribution of sex (50% female and 50% male).
The age distribution revealed that the majority of patients (44%) were young adults, particularly within the 20–35 year age group, followed by those aged 36–45 years (18%). Additionally, a significant proportion of patients (87%) were classified as "Re-attendance", indicating that many patients returned for follow-up visits during the study period. This pattern highlights the high burden of mental health conditions in young adults  and the recurrent nature of care-seeking behavior by the mental health patients.


```{r}
#| label: tbl-table1-demo
#| tbl-cap: "Socio-demographic characteristics of patients"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false



# Summary statistics for socio-demographics
tb1 <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>% 
  select(mh_age_group, mh_sex, mh_year, mh_classification,
         mh_patient_category, mh_depression, mh_anxiety) %>%
   mutate(
    mh_year = as.factor(mh_year),
    mh_depression = as.factor(mh_depression),
    mh_anxiety = as.factor(mh_anxiety)
   ) %>% 
tbl_summary(
                         label = list(mh_sex = "Sex",
                                      mh_depression = "Depression",
                                      mh_anxiety = "Anxiety",
                                      mh_year  = "Year of visit",  
                                      mh_classification = "Classification",
                                      mh_patient_category = "Nationality",
                                      mh_age_group = "Age group"),
                         type = all_dichotomous() ~ "categorical",
                          missing_text = "(Missing)",
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  #add_n() %>%
  #add_overall(last = T) %>% 
  bold_labels() 


table1 <- as_gt(tb1) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = c("left", "right"),
      color = "black",
      weight = px(1.5)
    ),
    locations = list(cells_body(), cells_column_labels()) 
  ) %>% 
  
  tab_style(
  style = cell_fill(color = "navyblue"),
  locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "white"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_options(
    table.font.names = "Arial",
    table.font.size = px(14),
    data_row.padding = px(5),
    table.background.color = "white",
    #column_labels.background.color = "lightgray"
    )
 
table1

t1 <- gtsave(table1, filename = here::here("outputs", "table1.png"))

#request_text = "Use the image t1 from gtsummary package above to provide a 3-sentence insight about the socio-demographic characteristics of patients diagnosed with depression and anxiety in Uganda. Focus on key patterns and trends that can be observed from the table, such as age distribution"

#table1_log = gemini_image(image = t1, prompt = request_text,  maxOutputTokens = 5000)

#narrative <- gemini_chat(paste0( "Provide a 3-sentence insight about this table:", table1_log))
#print(narrative)

```



# DEPRESSION AND ANXIETY 
For the rest of the analysis, we focus on the two target diagnoses of depression and anxiety, the most prevalent mental health conditions and focus of the research. 

## Exploratory analysis 
@tbl-demo-table2 presents the distribution of patient demographic characteristics with respect to diagnosis. A higher proportion of the patients diagnosed with depression were females (55%) compared to males (45%). Similarly, the proportion of patients diagnosed with anxiety was also higher among females (60%) compared to males (40%) indicating a higher burden which is consistent with global trends in mental health epidemiology. Majority of patients diagnosed with depression and anxiety fall within the 20-35 years age group indicating a higher burden among young adults. 
 
```{r}
#| label: tbl-demo-table1
#| tbl-cap: "Demographic characteristics of patients"
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| warning: false

target_data <- read_csv(here("data", "data_dep_anx.csv")) %>% 
    mutate(visits_cat = case_when(
    mh_visits_total == 1 ~ "1 visit",
    mh_visits_total == 2  ~ "2 visits",
    mh_visits_total == 3 ~ "3 visits",
    mh_visits_total == 4 ~ "4 visits",
    mh_visits_total >= 5 ~ "5+ visits"),
    mh_age_group = fct_relevel(mh_age_group, c("< 5yrs", "5-9", "10-14", "15-19",
                                        "20-35", "36-45", "46-55",  " > 55") 
  ))
tbx <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>% 
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes"|mh_anxiety == "Yes") %>% 
  select(mh_age_group, mh_sex, mh_year, mh_classification,
         mh_patient_category) %>%
   mutate(
    mh_year = as.factor(mh_year)
   ) %>% 
tbl_summary(
                         label = list(mh_sex = "Sex",
                                      mh_year  = "Year of visit",  
                                      mh_classification = "Classification",
                                      mh_patient_category = "Nationality",
                                      mh_age_group = "Age group"),
                         type = all_dichotomous() ~ "categorical",
                          missing_text = "(Missing)",
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  #add_n() %>%
  #add_overall(last = T) %>% 
  bold_labels() 


table1_f <- as_gt(tbx) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = c("left", "right"),
      color = "black",
      weight = px(1.5)
    ),
    locations = list(cells_body(), cells_column_labels()) 
  ) %>% 
  
  tab_style(
  style = cell_fill(color = "navyblue"),
  locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "white"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_options(
    table.font.names = "Arial",
    table.font.size = px(14),
    data_row.padding = px(5),
    table.background.color = "white",
    #column_labels.background.color = "lightgray"
    )
 
table1_f
```



## Association with diagnosis
```{r}
#| label: tbl-demo-table2
#| tbl-cap: "Associations between socio-demographic characteristics and mental health diagnosis"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false


# bi-variate associations with diagnosis
tb2 <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
  mutate(mh_depression= if_else(mh_depression == "Yes", "Depression", mh_depression)) %>%
   select(mh_sex, mh_age_group, mh_classification, mh_depression) %>% 
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = mh_depression, 
                         label = list( mh_sex = "Sex",
                                      mh_depression = "Depression",
                                      mh_classification = "Classification",
                                      mh_age_group = "Age group"),
                          missing_text = "(Missing)",
                          #digits = all_categorical() ~ 2,
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  #add_n() %>%
  #add_overall(last = T) %>% 
  bold_labels() 

#Table for Anxiety
tb2_b <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_anxiety == "Yes") %>%
  mutate(mh_anxiety= if_else(mh_anxiety == "Yes", "Anxiety", mh_anxiety)) %>%
  select(mh_sex, mh_age_group, mh_classification, mh_anxiety) %>% 
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = mh_anxiety, 
                         label = list( mh_sex = "Sex",
                                       mh_classification = "Classification",
                                       mh_age_group = "Age group"),
                         missing_text = "(Missing)",
                         #digits = all_categorical() ~ 2,
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  bold_labels() 

#merge the two tables
table2 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(tb2, tb2_b),
  tab_spanner = FALSE
)



table2 <- as_gt(table2) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = c("left", "right"),
      color = "black",
      weight = px(1.5)
    ),
    locations = list(cells_body(), cells_column_labels()) 
  ) %>% 
  
  tab_style(
  style = cell_fill(color = "navyblue"),
  locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "white"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_options(
    table.font.names = "Arial",
    table.font.size = px(14),
    data_row.padding = px(5),
    table.background.color = "white",
    #column_labels.background.color = "lightgray"
    )
 
table2


```


```{r}
#| label: age-group-plot
#| fig-cap: "Depression by patient age group and sex"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| fig.width: 12
#| out-width: "100%"

 # We will use the fresh theme from ggthemr to plot the age distribution graph
ggthemr('fresh')

age_depression <- target_data %>%
   distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
  filter(!is.na(mh_age_group)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(mh_age_group)) +
  geom_bar(na.rm = T, fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = after_stat(count)),
    stat = "count",
    vjust = -0.05,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Age group", y = "No. of patients", title = "Depression by patient age group") +
  #scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 35000, by = 5000)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 40000)) +
  #coord_flip() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )


sex_depression <- target_data %>%
   distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
  filter(!is.na(mh_sex)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(mh_sex)) +
  geom_bar(na.rm = T, fill = "#FF1493") +
  #scale_fill_manual(values = c("seagreen4", "lightblue3")) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = after_stat(count)),
    stat = "count",
    vjust = -0.05,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Sex", y = "", title = "Depression by pateint sex") +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 40000)) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 40000)) +
  #coord_flip() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

age_depression + sex_depression + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

```


```{r}
#| label: age_group_sex_plot
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| fig.width: 12
target_data %>%
   distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mh_age_group)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(mh_sex)) %>%
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
ggplot(
       aes(mh_age_group, fill = mh_sex)
)+
  geom_bar(na.rm = T, 
           position = "dodge") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#FF1493", "#4682B4")) +
  #scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "cividis") + 
  geom_text(
    aes(label = after_stat(count)), 
    stat = "count",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    vjust = -0.05, 
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold",  
    color = "black")+
  labs( x= "Age group",
        y = "No. of patients",
        fill = "Sex") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 20000, by = 5000))+
  scale_x_discrete(expand = c(0,0))+
  #coord_flip()+
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    #legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

```
 
 
```{r}
#| label: tbl-demo-table3
#| tbl-cap: "Depression patients across facilities"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false


# bi-variate associations with diagnosis
tb3 <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
  mutate(mh_depression= if_else(mh_depression == "Yes", "Depression", mh_depression)) %>%
   select(mh_organisation_unit_name, mh_depression) %>% 
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = mh_depression, 
                         label = list( mh_organisation_unit_name = "Hospital",
                                      mh_depression = "Depression"),
                         sort = list(mh_organisation_unit_name ~ "frequency"),
                          missing_text = "(Missing)",
                          #digits = all_categorical() ~ 2,
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  #add_n() %>%
  #add_overall(last = T) %>% 
  bold_labels() 

#Table for Anxiety
tb3_b <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_anxiety == "Yes") %>%
  mutate(mh_anxiety= if_else(mh_anxiety == "Yes", "Anxiety", mh_anxiety)) %>%
  select(mh_organisation_unit_name, mh_anxiety) %>% 
  gtsummary::tbl_summary(by = mh_anxiety, 
                         label = list( mh_organisation_unit_name = "Hospital"),
                         missing_text = "(Missing)",
                         sort = list(mh_organisation_unit_name ~ "frequency"),
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  bold_labels() 



table3 <- as_gt(tb3) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = c("left", "right"),
      color = "black",
      weight = px(1.5)
    ),
    locations = list(cells_body(), cells_column_labels()) 
  ) %>% 
  
  tab_style(
  style = cell_fill(color = "navyblue"),
  locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "white"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_options(
    table.font.names = "Arial",
    table.font.size = px(14),
    data_row.padding = px(5),
    table.background.color = "white",
    #column_labels.background.color = "lightgray"
    )
 
table3

low = (min(target_data$mh_visits_total) +1)
up = max(target_data$mh_visits_total) 
```

# LONGITUDINAL DATA ANALYSIS OF ANXIETY AND DEPRESSION PATIENT OUTCOMES

Over the three-year study period, some patients made repeated hospital visits ranging from **`r low` ** to **`r up` ** visits as presented in @tbl-visits-distribution. However a higher percentage of patients (83%) had only one visit during the study period. This pattern of visit frequency suggests that while a significant proportion of patients may have sought care for their mental health conditions, many did not return for follow-up visits, which could indicate challenges in continuity of care or barriers to accessing ongoing treatment.


## Visit frequency

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-visits-distribution
#| tbl-cap: "Hospital visits frequency"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false

tb5<- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  #filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
  mutate(mh_visits_total = as.factor(mh_visits_total)) %>%
  select(mh_visits_total) %>% 
  gtsummary::tbl_summary( 
                         label = list( mh_visits_total = "Hospital visits"
                                    ),
                          missing_text = "(Missing)",
                          #digits = all_categorical() ~ 2,
                         statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                                          all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)")) %>% 
  bold_labels() 

table5 <- as_gt(tb5) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = c("left", "right"),
      color = "black",
      weight = px(1.5)
    ),
    locations = list(cells_body(), cells_column_labels()) 
  ) %>% 
  
  tab_style(
  style = cell_fill(color = "navyblue"),
  locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(color = "white"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.font.names = "Arial",
    table.font.size = px(11),
    data_row.padding = px(2.5)
    )

table5 

```

:::

::: {.column width="5%"}

:::

::: {.column width="65%"}

```{r}
#| label: fig-visits__diagnosis
#| fig-cap: "Visit frequency for depression and anxiety patients"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| fig.width: 8
#| fig.height: 10


visits_dep <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=visits_cat)) +
  geom_bar(na.rm = T, fill = "#459395") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = after_stat(count)),
    stat = "count",
     hjust = -0.05,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Total hospital visits", y = "", 
       title = "Visit frequency for depression patients") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 60000)) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

visits_anx <- target_data %>% 
  distinct(patient_id, .keep_all = T) %>%
  filter(mh_anxiety == "Yes") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=visits_cat)) +
  geom_bar(na.rm = T, fill = "#d35400") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = after_stat(count)),
    stat = "count",
    hjust = -0.05,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Total hospital visits", y = "No. of patients", 
       title = "Visit frequency for anxiety patients") +
  #coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 50000)) +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

visits_dep + visits_anx + plot_layout(nrow = 2)
```

:::

:::

```{r}
#| label: visit-frequency-plot
#| fig-cap: "Visit frequency for depression patients"
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| warning: false
#| fig.width: 8


target_data <- target_data %>% 
  filter(mh_depression == "Yes"| mh_anxiety == "Yes") %>% 
  filter(mh_year >=2022 & mh_year <=2024) %>% 
  mutate(
  facility = gsub(
    pattern = "Regional Referral Hospital",
    replacement = "RRH",
    x = mh_organisation_unit_name )
  ) 

  
target_data %>% 
  group_by(mh_year, mh_month) %>% 
  mutate(mh_year = as.factor(mh_year)) %>%
  summarise(load= n()) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=mh_month, y=load, group = mh_year, colour = mh_year)) +
  geom_line( size=1) +
  geom_point( size=2) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 1:12, 
    labels = month.abb)+
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,3500), 
                     breaks = seq(0,3500, by=500)) +
  labs(title="Monthly patient visit patterns by year",
       x="",
       y="Number of Visits") +
  theme_minimal()


```

# Visit history
```{r}
#| label: visit-history
#| include: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

# How many of the depression patients had their 1st visit in the study period
# For those with visits> 1, how many have visit 1 as a new-attendance?


```
